name: AI Persona & Automated Publishing Playbook
goal: Ship a consistent, legally compliant character, a resilient automation loop with secure API integrations, strict human-in-the-loop checkpoints for high-risk content, and a credible public launch.
description: Phased playbook: identity and policy → pipeline and scheduling → safety and QA → soft launch and scale. Expanded to include IP compliance, prompt injection defense, and data feedback loops. Dates dynamically shifted to start April 1, 2026.

tasks.title: Audience, boundaries, and positioning
tasks.content:
Phase: Identity & Policy

Define who this is for and what it will never pretend to be.

Deliverables:
- Primary audience definition and the core "job to be done" (entertain, educate, support).
- Explicit out-of-bounds topics (health, finance, politics, minors).
- Rules of engagement for handling mistakes, disagreements, and refusal protocols.
- Competitive matrix noting tones to echo or actively avoid.

Done when:
- A one-page positioning memo is locked and approved by brand stakeholders.

tasks.title: Visual and voice style guide
tasks.content:
Phase: Identity & Policy

Lock a reference set before generating at scale to ensure cross-platform consistency.

Deliverables:
- Visual mood board: 10–20 locked reference images detailing palette, framing, and wardrobe rules.
- Output format requirements per channel (aspect ratios, resolution, length caps).
- Voice specs: 3–5 "golden" sample scripts marking desired cadence, banned jargon, and sign-offs.
- Audio architecture: Decision on synthetic vs. recorded human voice, establishing the minimum quality bar.

Done when:
- A comprehensive style guide document exists that a new contractor or AI agent could accurately prompt from.

tasks.title: Behavior spec and knowledge sources (RAG)
tasks.content:
Phase: Identity & Policy

Turn personality into strict system instructions the LLM can follow consistently.

Deliverables:
- Master system prompt: Role, overarching goals, format defaults, and strict citation rules.
- Few-shot examples library covering common interactions (replies, threads, video hooks, apologies).
- Curated knowledge base: The exact files or retrieval (RAG) sources the persona is allowed to treat as ground truth.
- Escalation tree: Trigger conditions for deferring to a human, citing external sources, or admitting "I don't know."

Done when:
- You have run 20 manual edge-case prompts against the spec with zero critical hallucinations.

tasks.title: Legal, compliance, and brand channels
tasks.content:
Phase: Identity & Policy

Secure profiles and ensure legal viability before any automation goes live.

Deliverables:
- IP Check: Verify commercial use rights for chosen foundational models, voice clones, and image generators.
- Profile Setup: Consistent handles, bios, and profile art across all target surfaces.
- Disclosure Compliance: Draft clear bot-labeling and automation disclosures that meet platform-specific TOS.
- Appeal Path: Documented contact routes for platform restriction appeals.

Done when:
- Profiles are complete with human-readable "about this AI" blurbs, and legal/IP risks are formally accepted.

tasks.title: Automation map & API verification
tasks.content:
Phase: Pipeline & Ops

Draw the architecture and verify access before wiring tools together.

Deliverables:
- Trigger definitions (schedule, RSS, manual approval webhook).
- State management plan detailing where data lives (database, queue) and retry logic for failed steps.
- Secret management: Secure storage plan for API keys and credentials.
- Rate limit and cost cap documentation per vendor to prevent runaway billing.
- Platform API access verification (ensuring you have the necessary read/write tiers for chosen social platforms).

Done when:
- Architecture diagram is reviewed by a third party to spot failure points, and all required API keys are active in a staging environment.

tasks.title: Implement draft → asset → publish path
tasks.content:
Phase: Pipeline & Ops

Build the minimum viable pipeline that produces one real post type end-to-end.

Deliverables:
- Version control for prompts/templates allowing instant rollbacks.
- Metadata storage tracking model version, generation seed, and timestamp for every output.
- Scheduling mechanism with timezone awareness and per-channel optimization.

Done when:
- One complete cycle (ideation to staged draft) runs seamlessly without manual UI intervention, fully logged.

tasks.title: Safety layer: moderation and blocklists
tasks.content:
Phase: Safety & QA

Design the defense mechanisms for when models inevitably drift or hallucinate.

Deliverables:
- Automated pre-publish checks: Classifiers, regex keyword blocklists, and banned URL filters.
- Human review queue explicitly for the high-risk topics defined in Phase 1.
- Kill switch protocol: A documented method to halt all automated publishing globally in under 5 minutes.
- Data retention policy for generated media and moderation logs.

Done when:
- A tabletop exercise successfully simulates three severe output failures, testing the kill switch and deletion protocols.

tasks.title: Dry runs, red-teaming, and seed batch
tasks.content:
Phase: Safety & QA

Stress-test the system and build a buffer before the public cares.

Deliverables:
- Red-teaming: Actively attempt prompt injection or adversarial attacks to break the persona's boundaries.
- End-to-end dry runs at 2x expected volume to monitor latency, cost, and API timeouts.
- Media QA: Inspect outputs for visual artifacts, audio sync issues, or missing watermarks.
- Generation of 10–20 varied, approved posts kept unpublished as a regression testing set.

Done when:
- All P1/P0 blockers are ticketed and resolved, or temporarily gated behind strict manual approval.

tasks.title: Soft launch and engagement playbook
tasks.content:
Phase: Launch

Warm up the accounts organically before leaning heavily on automation algorithms.

Deliverables:
- Initial human-driven activity plan (follows, non-automated replies, shares).
- Teaser content deployment schedule with associated creative assets.
- Engagement policy defining exact scenarios where the persona replies automatically versus when a human steps in.

Done when:
- The first week of public activity is fully scheduled, with human owners assigned to non-automated community management.

tasks.title: Go-live, monitoring, and analytics
tasks.content:
Phase: Launch

Transition to steady state with active dashboard monitoring.

Deliverables:
- On-call rotation for the first 48 hours of live automation.
- Live dashboard tracking core KPIs: reach, engagement rate, cost per post, and moderation queue volume.
- Setup of a weekly 30-minute review cadence to analyze hits, misses, and prompt drift.
- Changelog initialized for tracking adjustments to system prompts or automation logic.

Done when:
- Live mode is activated, dashboards are populating data, and the rollback path has been successfully tested in production.

tasks.title: Live formats & continuous optimization
tasks.content:
Phase: Scale

Expand capabilities only after batch content processing proves highly stable.

Deliverables:
- Latency budget requirements for streaming or real-time text generation.
- Fallback strategies for AV pipeline failures during live broadcasts.
- Strict live-chat moderation tools (slow mode, automated profanity filters).
- Feedback loop integration: mechanism to feed high-performing post data back into the "few-shot" examples library to continuously improve model outputs.

Done when:
- A pilot live stream is completed, followed by a formal postmortem deciding the go/no-go for regular live programming.
